Ad Text:
Overview


Developers are working hard to build our new reality, and the Azure Experience & Ecosystems Engineering team is here to help. The team's product, Microsoft Learn, offers learning paths, content, and interactive experiences that help people develop the skills that in-demand jobs require. As such, we contribute directly to Microsoft's mission to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more.

As a Software Engineer II in Azure Experience & Ecosystems Engineering team, you will build the Microsoft Learn product which will positively impact how people experience Microsoft's technology. You will be responsible for building Content and Learning experiences through constant innovation, design, and implementing state-of-the-art systems. This opportunity will allow you to learn from unique engineering challenges in AI, frontend and backend services and leverage AI to shape Microsoft Learn into a trusted, personalized knowledge platform that meets developers where they are.

Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond.

Responsibilities


Works with product management, design and data analyst teams to determine user requirements for a set of features.
Contributes to the identification of dependencies, and the development of design documents for a product area with little oversight.
Creates and implements code for a product, service, or feature, reusing code as applicable.
Contributes to efforts to break down larger work items into smaller work items and provides estimation.
Acts as a Designated Responsible Individual (DRI) working on-call to monitor system/product feature/service for degradation, downtime, or interruptions and gains approval to restore system/product/service for simple problems.
Remains current in skills by investing time and effort into staying abreast of current developments that will improve the availability, reliability, efficiency, observability, and performance of products while also driving consistency in monitoring and operations at scale.

Qualifications


Required Qualifications: 

Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science or related technical field AND 2+ years technical engineering experience with coding in languages including, but not limited to, C, C++, C#, Java, JavaScript, or Python OR equivalent experience. 
2+ years of professional experience in cloud service development using one or more of these technologies: Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP), Hypertext Markup Language (HTML), JavaScript, Cascading Style Sheets (CSS), .NET, Node.js, Representational State Transfer Application Programming Interfaces (REST APIs).
1+ year(s) of demonstrated problem-solving, design, and debugging skills, with a passion for quality, performance, and engineering excellence.

Other Requirements: 

Ability to meet Microsoft, customer and/or government security screening requirements are required for this role. These requirements include, but are not limited to the following specialized security screenings: Microsoft Cloud Background Check: This position will be required to pass the Microsoft Cloud Background Check upon hire/transfer and every two years thereafter. 

Preferred Qualifications:  

Experience with cloud-native architecture, microservices, and asynchronous workflows. 
Experience with agent-based systems, workflow engines, or declarative programming models is highly desirable.
Experience with AI-assisted coding tools (e.g., GitHub Copilot) and large language models (LLMs) such as GPT or Claude Sonnet.
Experience collaborating closely with product and design to shape abstractions and reference implementations that scale across use cases.

Job Title: Senior Azure Cloud Customer Experience Engineer
Ad Text:
Overview


Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond.

Every minute of every day, customers stake their entire business and reputation on Microsoft Cloud. The Azure Customer Experience (CXP) team believes that when we meet our high standards for quality and reliability, our customers win. If we falter, our customers fail their end-customers. Our vision is to turn Microsoft Cloud customers into fans.

We are the ACES Strategic team (Advanced Cloud Engineering & Supportability), a global engineering team in Azure CXP and we are focused on Strategic Azure Customers. We are customer-obsessed problem-solvers. We orchestrate and drive deep engagements in areas like Incident Management, Problem Management, Support, Resiliency, and empowering the customers. We represent the customer and amplify customer voice with Azure Engineering connecting to the quality vision for Azure. We innovate and find ways to scale our learning across our customer base. Diversity and inclusion are central to who we are, how we work, and what we enable our customers to achieve. We know that empowering our customers starts with empowering our team to show up authentically, work in ways that are best for them, and achieve their career goals.


Are you constantly customer-obsessed and passionate about solving complex technical problems? Do you take pride in enhancing customer experience through innovation? If the answer is Yes, then join us and surround yourself with people who are passionate about cloud computing and believe that extraordinary support is critical to customer success.

As a customer focused Advanced Cloud Engineer, you are the primary engineering contact accountable for your customer’s support experience on Azure. You will drive resolution of critical and complex problems, support key customer projects on Azure, and be the voice of the customer within Azure. In this role, you will work in partnership with Customer Success Account Managers, Cloud Solution Architects, Technical Support Engineers, and Azure engineering with our mission to turn Azure customers into fans with world-class engineering-led support experience.

Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond.

Responsibilities


Technically Oriented
•    Track customer incidents, engage with strategic customers and partners to understand issues, contribute to troubleshooting through diagnostics, communicate progress and next steps to customers with a focus on reducing time taken to mitigate critical incidents.
•    Use engineering and support tools, customer telemetry and/or direct customer input to detect and flag trends/patterns of issues in the products or products misuse across customers. Develop and share insights and best practices with customers.
•    Recommend and help develop training/readiness modules (e.g., mentoring, leading triages, content creation, brown bag sessions, blogs, quality assurance checks). Acts as a mentor, shares knowledge, and contributes to the content and readiness strategy for the team.
Customer Solution Lifecycle Management
•    Serve as a connecting point between the product team and customers throughout the engagement lifecycle, engage with customers to understand their business and availability needs, develop and offer proactive guidance on designing configurations and deploying solutions on Azure.
•    Handle complex escalations on customer issues from the customer or support or field teams, conduct impact analysis, help customers with answers to their technical questions, and serve as an escalation resource in areas of subject matter expertise.
•    Conduct in-depth root cause analysis of issues and translates findings into opportunities for improvement and track and drive them as repair items.
Relationship/Experience Management
•    Act as voice of customers and synthesize product feedback from strategic customers to provide input on business plans developed by the product groups. Identify customer usage patterns and drive resolutions on reoccurring issues with product groups. Close the feedback loop with the customers on product features.
•    Partner with other teams (e.g., program managers, software engineers, product, customer service support teams), prioritize, unblock, and resolve critical customer issues.
•    Collaborate with stakeholders to deliver solutions to strategic customers and develop mechanisms that improve customer health and reduce the turnover time.

Qualifications


•    Bachelor's Degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Information Technology (IT), Data Analytics/Science, Artificial Intelligence (AI), or related field AND 4+ years experience in technology industry, cloud, technical support, and/or customer experience engineering 
   
o    OR equivalent experience. 
•    4+ years of demonstrated experience supporting and troubleshooting enterprise level, mission-critical applications and infrastructure resolving complex issues/situations and driving technical resolution across cross-functional organizations.
•    2+ years of Experience with being on-call and driving mitigation for mission critical incidents.

•    The ability to meet Microsoft, customer and/or government security screening requirements are required for this role. These requirements include but are not limited to the following specialized security screenings: Microsoft Cloud Background Check: This position will be required to pass the Microsoft Cloud background check upon hire/transfer and every two years thereafter.

•    Technical Skills: Demonstrated hands-on experience with Subject Matter Expert level expertise in one or more of the following Cloud technologies:
o    Core IaaS: Compute (Windows/Linux), Storage, Networking, Kubernetes, High Availability
o    Data Platform and Bigdata: Azure SQL DB, Cosmos DB, PostgreSQL on Azure, Azure Data Bricks, Azure Data Factory, AI/ML
o    Azure PaaS Services: App Services, Azure Functions, Redis Cache, and Event Hub.
o    Monitoring technologies: Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, Grafana, Datadog, Confluent and similar technologies.
•    Communication skills: ability to empathize with customers and convey confidence. Able to explain highly technical issues to varied audiences. Able to prioritize and advocate customer’s needs to the proper channels. Take ownership and work towards a resolution.
•    Customer Obsession: Passion for customers and focus on delivering the right customer experience.
•    Growth Mindset: Openness and ability to learn new skills and technologies in a fast-paced environment.




This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.

Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. MMS is embarking on a major multi‑year technology transformation, rebuilding the core digital foundations that power some of Australia’s most recognised employee benefits and salary packaging brands. We’re modernising our platforms end‑to‑end and shifting to cloud‑native, scalable, event‑driven architectures that will set the business up for the future.

As part of this journey, you’ll join a team designing and delivering modern distributed systems; re‑engineering legacy workloads into contemporary cloud patterns; and solving complex integration challenges across secure, highly available enterprise platforms. It’s an opportunity to help shape a flagship transformation and contribute to the building of solutions for millions of customers.

If you enjoy building internal platforms, automating infrastructure, and making opinionated engineering decisions that scale, this role is for you.

As a Platform Engineer, you will design and operate the cloud platform that powers our financial services, enabling engineering teams to deliver secure, reliable software from local development through to mission-critical production.

This role is for a software/cloud platform engineer working with infrastructure automation and developer platforms (not a civil, structural, or mechanical engineering role).

How you’ll make a difference

You’ll be responsible for building and evolving the cloud platform that enables secure, scalable software delivery across the organisation.

You should enjoy writing code to automate infrastructure and platforms rather than manually configuring systems.

At MMS clear expectations help our people Be the Difference.  Your key responsibilities in this role will include: 

Design, implement and maintain Infrastructure as Code using Terraform

Design, build, and maintain CI/CD pipelines as code, enabling fully automated, reliable deployments across multiple environments

Develop reusable platform components and automation to improve developer experience and consistency

Implement platform engineering practices to standardise environments from development through production

Partner with application engineering teams to improve build pipelines, deployment patterns, and platform capabilities

Embed security, governance, and automation into the platform by design

Diagnose and resolve complex issues across cloud infrastructure, platform tooling, and deployment pipelines

 To be considered for this role you will have: 

Experience building reusable Terraform modules, platform tooling, or infrastructure abstractions that enable multiple development teams

Experience working in environments where infrastructure changes are delivered through pull requests and automated pipelines

Experience building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines (not just using them)

Proven hands-on experience in platform engineering, DevOps engineering, or cloud infrastructure engineering roles

Strong experience with Azure PaaS services

Experience designing cloud-native platforms supporting multiple engineering teams

Solid understanding of DevOps practices, Git-based workflows, and automated delivery

Ability to work closely with developers to improve build, deployment, and platform capabilities

Experience embedding security, compliance, and governance into cloud platforms

 What we can offer you:

Novated leasing benefits and discounts

12 weeks paid parental leave and access to our Parents Portal

Comprehensive learning and development opportunities to support your career growth

Sonder digital wellbeing platform, providing personalised support 24/7, plus annual flu vaccinations

Default Income Protection Insurance reimbursed for members of the MMS Default Super Fund

Exempt Employee Share Plan

Embracing our value of Everyone Matters we hold a collective commitment to foster an environment where all differences are valued and respected. 

We encourage individuals from all backgrounds including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, those caring for someone or living with a disability, LGBTQIA+ and culturally diverse applicants to apply.

We embrace hybrid working and welcome conversations about flexibility. 

We value the skills and attributes veterans can bring to our organisation.    

 Please note all successful candidates will undergo background checks (including criminal history and ASIC checks) and an NDIS Workers Screening Check if appropriate.  All information provided will be treated confidentially. 

 We acknowledge Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples as the Traditional Custodians of the lands where we live, learn and work.

 If you identify as a person living with disability and require adjustments to our recruitment process, please contact us at mmsgrouprecruitment@mmsg.com.au

Overview


In alignment with our Microsoft values, we are committed to cultivating an inclusive work environment for all employees to positively impact our culture every day and we need you as a Critical Environment Industrial Controls Systems Engineer (CE IE).   In the CEIE role, you will have an opportunity to work alongside a dynamic team of industry professionals across the Information Technology (IT) and Critical Environments (CE) spectrum within Microsoft 24x7x365 mission-critical datacenters.   Microsoft’s Cloud Operations & Innovation (CO+I) is the engine that powers our cloud services.

As a CO+I CE IE, you will perform a key role in delivering the core infrastructure and foundational technologies for Microsoft's online services including Bing, Office 365, Xbox, OneDrive, and the Microsoft Azure platform. As a group, CO+I is focused on the personal and professional development for all employees and offers trainings and growth opportunities including Career Rotation Programs, Diversity & Inclusion trainings and events, and professional certifications.   Our infrastructure is comprised of a large global portfolio of more than 200 datacenters in 32 countries and millions of servers.

Our foundation is built upon and managed by a team of subject matter experts working to support services for more than 1 billion customers and 20 million businesses in over 90 countries worldwide.   With environmental sustainability and optimization at the forefront of our datacenter design and operations, we continue to grow and evolve as we meet the ever-changing business demands that hold Microsoft as a world-class cloud provider.   Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more.

As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond.

Digital Six is a 40-person marketing agency based in Brisbane, managing a large client

base across Google Ads, SEO, and social media. Our team delivers structured weekly

performance reviews, client reporting, and ongoing strategy development across every

account.


We are building an AI-powered agentic operating model that will transform how we

onboard clients, manage campaigns, and deliver results. This is not a chatbot project or

a simple automation layer - we are building a system of autonomous AI agents that

research, analyse, recommend, and execute across every client account, with human

oversight at key decision points.




This is a greenfield build with detailed architecture documentation, workflow designs,

and a production codebase scaffold already in place. We need a senior engineer who

can take this from plan to production, contribute to architectural decisions alongside the

existing technical leadership, and build a system that scales reliably across our entire

client base.




The Role

You will be the primary engineer building Digital Six’s AI agent platform. You will work

closely with the founder and a technical advisor who reviews architecture and code

quality. You will have significant input into technical decisions and the freedom to

propose improvements, while operating within an established architectural direction.

This is a hands-on build role - you will write production code daily.




What you will build:

• AI agents that automate key operational workflows across the agency - starting with

client onboarding and expanding into campaign management, reporting, and client

communication.

• Integrations with the platforms our team uses daily - Google Ads, GA4, Search

Console, Meta, HubSpot, Slack, ClickUp, Zoom, and others - so agents can pull data,

take actions, and surface insights autonomously.

• A call intelligence layer that processes client call transcripts, extracts structured context,

and feeds it into the agent ecosystem as long-term memory.

• Approval and oversight workflows that keep humans in the loop at the right moments -

not everything should be automated, and knowing where to draw that line is part of the

job.

• The underlying platform: agent orchestration, semantic memory, observability, and the

infrastructure to run it all reliably at scale.

The first project is our client onboarding pipeline, with a clear roadmap beyond that.

This is an ongoing role building out the AI layer across the entire agency.


Who You Are

• You have built and shipped production AI/ML systems - not just prototypes or proof-of-

concepts. You understand the difference between a demo and a system that runs

reliably at scale.

• You collaborate well with technical leadership. You can take architectural direction, but

you also flag gaps, propose alternatives, and bring your own experience to the table.

• You think in systems, not scripts. You naturally consider failure modes, retry logic,

observability, and what happens at 3am when an API rate-limits across hundreds of

concurrent client runs.

• You can context-switch between infrastructure work (Supabase schemas, Cloud Run

deployment, CI/CD) and AI work (prompt engineering, agent orchestration, tool use

patterns) in the same day.

• You are pragmatic about AI - you know when an LLM is the right tool and when a

A simple database query or rule-based check is better. You optimise for cost and reliability,

not novelty.


Required Skills & Experience

Core Programming & Architecture

• TypeScript/Node.js - production-level. This is the primary language for the entire

system.

• Python - working proficiency. Used for data processing, scripting, and some tooling.

• API design and integration - RESTful APIs, webhook handling, OAuth 2.0 flows. You

will be connecting to 10+ external platforms.

• Event-driven architecture - webhooks, message queues, async processing patterns.

• Understanding of state machines and workflow orchestration patterns.




AI; Agent Frameworks

• LangGraph - multi-step agent orchestration with state management, conditional

branching, parallel execution (fan-out/fan-in), and human-in-the-loop checkpoints. This is

the core orchestration layer.

• Claude API (Anthropic) - tool use pattern where Claude decides which tools to call and

in what order, rather than following scripted sequences. Understanding of prompt

engineering, structured outputs, and multi-turn reasoning chains.

• LLM fundamentals - token management, context windows, model selection (Haiku for

speed, Sonnet for balance, Opus for complex reasoning), cost optimisation at scale.

• Vector embeddings and semantic search - pgvector for storing and retrieving

contextual memory so agents can recall relevant historical information.

• Langfuse - agent observability, prompt tracing, evaluation pipelines, and debugging

multi-step agent workflows.




Infrastructure; Cloud

• Supabase - PostgreSQL database, pgvector extension, Storage (for generated

documents), Edge Functions (for webhook handling), Row Level Security, and Auth.

• Google Cloud Platform (GCP) - Cloud Run (containerised agent compute), Cloud

Scheduler (cron triggers for recurring workflows), Artifact Registry, Cloud Logging.

• Docker - containerisation for Cloud Run deployment.

• CI/CD pipelines - automated testing, deployment, and rollback.

• Environment management - secrets, configuration, staging vs. production.


Highly Desirable (Not Required)

• Digital marketing domain knowledge - understanding of Google Ads campaign

structures, SEO fundamentals, social media advertising, and marketing KPIs.

• Experience with AI-assisted development tools - Cursor, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot,

or similar. We expect heavy use of these tools to accelerate development.

• Web scraping and crawling - for CRO audits, competitor analysis, and brand extraction

from client websites.

• Document generation - programmatically creating professional reports (HTML, PDF, or

similar) from structured data.

• Experience building multi-tenant SaaS or agency-scale systems serving a large client

base from a single codebase.

Why now

If you are stuck building dashboards on top of unstable source data, patching metrics after the fact, or working somewhere that treats reporting as an end in itself — this is the alternative.

The mandate is simple: connect the firm's data sources, shape them into a reliable platform, expose them through trusted models and interfaces, and make that data usable by both people and AI-driven systems.

This is a build role, not a dashboard factory.




About ADLV Law

ADLV Law is a mid-market firm with interstate offices and serious internal infrastructure: automation, quality systems, and performance data designed to be used day-to-day, not exported into spreadsheets.

We already have meaningful operational data, document repositories, and reporting assets. The next step is turning those systems into a coherent internal data and knowledge platform that supports reporting, search, workflow, and agent-based access.




The role

You will sit close to the COO and leadership team and work across lawyers, operations, and business services.

Your job is to connect and structure our data sources, build a maintainable transformation and access layer, and deliver practical interfaces that let the business query, understand, and act on its data.

This includes structured data in operational systems, document and file repositories, and communication sources — including:

PostgreSQL and related relational data

Metabase reporting models and dashboards

SharePoint and Microsoft 365 data and files

Microsoft Graph API data (email, search-accessible content)

Other internal systems as required

You will design and maintain the layers that sit between those sources and end-user access — ingestion and sync, transformation and modelling, quality and governance, retrieval and search, and AI-ready access paths. 

Dashboards remain important, but they are only one output of the platform.




What you'll do

Connect and structure the platform

Review, document, and map source systems, schemas, lineage, and dependencies

Build and maintain ingestion and synchronisation processes across structured and unstructured data sources

Design a reliable data model and semantic layer for reporting, internal tools, and AI use cases

Define and document metric logic, business rules, and semantic conventions — a clear single source of truth

Write, test, and maintain transformation logic

Profile datasets to find gaps, inconsistencies, and quality issues

Write SQL-first transformations with Python where it earns its place

Build stable, reusable models that serve reporting, search, and agent access

Own reconciliation checks, edge-case handling, regression tests, and lightweight monitoring

Build practical access and interfaces

Build lightweight internal applications, APIs, and tools on top of the platform

Support retrieval and search access to document and file-based content

Enable safe, well-documented access paths for agents and internal AI workflows

Build Metabase dashboards where they serve real decisions — not as a default output

Keep it maintainable

Maintain documentation, version control, and a change process so the platform stays stable

Leave a trail someone else can follow. No black boxes.

Own QA across ingestion, transformation, and reporting layers




About you

You are not just a dashboard builder. You are someone who gets frustrated when data doesn't make sense and isn't satisfied until you understand why. You learn by doing, ask good questions, and care more about whether the business can actually use what you build than how sophisticated the stack looks.

You are comfortable moving across data modelling, transformation, retrieval, and lightweight application delivery. You can talk to a lawyer about what they need and translate it into something precise and buildable.

You measure success by whether the platform enables real decisions and workflows — not by the number of dashboards shipped.




What you need

Must-haves

Strong SQL — complex joins, CTEs, window functions, query optimisation

Strong Python for data work, transformation, integration, or application support

Experience building or maintaining ETL/ELT pipelines or equivalent ingestion workflows

Experience integrating APIs and external systems into internal data platforms

Experience transforming messy source data into reliable, documented, maintainable models

Ability to build practical internal tools or application-layer outputs on top of data

Clear documentation habits and strong versioning and change control instincts

Comfortable working with business stakeholders to turn ambiguous requests into precise outcomes

Nice to have

Exposure to dbt or dbt-style modelling and testing

Familiarity with Airbyte or similar ingestion tooling

Experience with Metabase or comparable BI tools

Some exposure to Microsoft Graph API, Microsoft 365, or SharePoint

Any experience with retrieval, search, or RAG-style patterns — even if experimental

Background in professional services, legal, accounting, or similar environments




What success looks like in 12 months

A stable internal platform connecting the firm's core structured and unstructured data sources

Reliable ingestion, transformation, and access patterns with documented ownership

A maintainable metrics and semantic layer used consistently across reporting and internal tools

Operational dashboards that are trusted and actually used

Practical search or retrieval access to document and knowledge sources where it improves workflows

Clear pathways for agents or AI tools to access approved data safely

Fewer one-off reporting requests because the business can self-serve

Clear visibility into matter economics and operational performance — with documented actions taken




What this role is not

A report factory or dashboard-only position

A spreadsheet rebuild service

A disconnected RAG experiment with no operational value

A black-box stack that nobody else can maintain

A situation where source-system issues are ignored and patched downstream forever




How to Apply

We’re reviewing applications as they come in, so early applications are encouraged. The role may close once a suitable candidate is appointed!

JOB SUMMARY

We are seeking a detail-oriented and technically strong Software QA Engineer - Medical Imaging to ensure the quality, reliability, and performance of our advanced imaging and analytics software. This role will play a critical part in validating analytic pipelines, including medical imaging workflows, and ensuring our products meet the highest standards of accuracy, scalability, and usability.

The ideal candidate will be motivated by working on complex, data-intensive systems and will bring hands-on experience in software quality assurance within medical imaging, neuroscience, or similarly regulated technical domains. You will collaborate closely with Engineering, Product, and Design teams to embed quality throughout the development lifecycle, from early design through deployment.

This position requires developing deep technical expertise in analytic and imaging pipelines, test automation frameworks, and CI/CD environments. The role will focus on designing scalable, maintainable testing strategies while monitoring system performance and proactively identifying quality gaps.

As a key member of a fast-growing, technology-driven organisation working at the intersection of medical imaging, analytics, and machine learning, you will have the opportunity to make an immediate impact on product quality and support the delivery of high-confidence solutions to clinical and research users.

ABOUT OMNISCIENT

Omniscient (o8t®) is the world leader in using AI to decode the human brain—a field known as connectomics. Our mission is to improve the lives of billions through connectomics. Today, Omniscient’s connectomic analysis platform, Quicktome®, generates personalized, patient-specific maps of an individual’s brain networks, or connectome. These critical insights inform prognosis and planning across neurologic conditions, from cranial surgery and neuro-oncology to stroke and beyond. Tomorrow, Omniscient is poised to revolutionize brain health and help conquer conditions such as Alzheimer’s disease and depression through truly personalized brain medicine.

Our products deliver these insights with enterprise-grade efficiency and usability, enabling broader access to vital subject-specific neurological insights.

Since founding, we have grown exponentially and achieved several world firsts with the development of the world’s first connectomic neurosurgical planning and visualization platform to be cleared by regulatory bodies. Omniscient recently expanded its product offering to include the first FDA-cleared neurological planning and visualization tool using resting-state fMRI, opening up new horizons for clinicians to assess brain connectivity and function in cases such as brain surgery, stroke, disorders of consciousness, and oncology.

With continued development, we intend to improve the lives of billions with both medical and non-medical products and services that drastically change how the human brain is understood, treated, and even enhanced.

RESPONSIBILITIES:

Design, develop, and execute comprehensive test plans, test cases, and automated test scripts for complex analytic and imaging software pipelines

Perform functional, regression, integration, performance, and unit testing across cloud-based software platforms

Define, implement, and continuously improve QA test frameworks for analytic pipelines, including medical imaging and neuroscience workflows

Validate medical imaging pipelines using formats such as DICOM and NIfTI, ensuring data accuracy, reliability, and performance

Monitor CI/CD pipelines, analyse test results, triage failures, and collaborate with engineering teams to resolve issues efficiently

Identify gaps in test coverage and implement scalable, maintainable testing strategies aligned with agile development practices

Track, document, and manage defects using bug-tracking tools, ensuring timely resolution and root-cause analysis

Work closely with Product, Engineering, and Design teams to ensure quality is embedded throughout the software development lifecycle

Participate in design reviews, sprint planning, and agile ceremonies, providing proactive feedback on quality, usability, and performance

Advocate for best practices in software quality engineering, automation, and continuous improvement

KEY REQUIREMENTS:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Life Sciences, Physics, or a related field

3+ years’ experience in a Software QA Engineer / Quality Engineering role

Experience testing medical imaging software, analytic pipelines, or data-intensive platforms

Strong automation and scripting skills in Python (preferred) and/or TypeScript

Solid knowledge of software testing methodologies, including functional, regression, integration, performance, and unit testing

Hands-on experience with medical imaging data formats such as DICOM and NIfTI

Experience with MRI imaging techniques, including fMRI (BOLD), DTI, and DWI

Strong understanding of image processing and validation for 2D/3D interactive visualization software

Experience working with CI/CD pipelines, automated test frameworks, and test infrastructure

Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and container technologies (Docker, Kubernetes)

Exposure to machine learning or AI-assisted testing is highly desirable

Understanding of regulatory and compliance standards in medical or healthcare software (e.g. ISO 13485, ISO 14971, HIPAA)

Strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced, agile environment

PERKS & BENEFITS

●        Competitive salary based on experience

●        Employee Share Option Plan

●        Flexible and hybrid working - we value work-life balance

A unique opportunity to ensure the quality and reliability of AI-driven neuroimaging analytics, supporting cutting-edge medical imaging software used in clinical and research environments.

Ad Text:
About Central Steel Build


In 1975, Central Steel Build began as a small family business with a humble, customer focused vision: to purpose build high quality sheds which would stand the test of time. We're now well into our second generation, and while our design and fabrication methods have become more sophisticated with cutting-edge modelling technology added to the mix, we've stayed true to our ideology of continuous improvement.

Our values shape us

Creating client value – we have a 'customer first' approach, driven by innovation, speed and attention to detail.

Working together – we demonstrate team energy by emulating a trusting, collaborative and results-focused workplace.

Continuous improvement – we see change as a smart opportunity to remain nimble, adaptable and accountable.

Being respectful – we foster a positive and humble mindset that listens and encourages.

Openly communicating – we have honest and fun two-way communication that is constructive and responsive.

Fast-Growing Manufacturing Business

Central Steel Build is embarking on a major AI, automation, and systems transformation. With a turnover of ~$65M and strong growth ambitions, we are implementing a full company-wide AI and automation roadmap.

 We are now searching for a strategic and hands-on technology leader who can own the architecture, build the data foundations, and lead our evolution into a modern, data-driven manufacturing organisation.

Reporting to the CEO, this role has enterprise‑wide accountability for architecture, data platforms, system integration, analytics, and AI governance.

The Role

The Head of Technology & Data is a strategic and hands‑on leader responsible for:

Designing and owning the enterprise architecture.

Establishing CSB’s AI operating model and governance.

Leading all system integration, automation, and data strategy.

Building and maintaining the Unified Analytics Layer.

Ensuring CSB has clean, reliable, real-time data to power AI agents, automated workflows, reporting, and future innovation.

Acting as the key executive partner to our external AI consultants while embedding capability inside CSB.

This is a role for a CTO-level leader who can think strategically AND tactically execute. 

This is a lead-by-doing and lead-by-thinking role. While you are not expected to build all solutions personally, you must be deeply comfortable with the detail – able to engage credibly with infrastructure architects, software engineers, data specialists, and vendors, and make informed decisions that balance technical excellence with business value.

About You

You will thrive in this role if you have:

Tertiary qualifications in Computer Science, Data Engineering, Information Technology, or a related field.

8+ years of experience across technology leadership, enterprise architecture, or data engineering

Experience integrating or architecting around NetSuite or a similar ERP

Strong hands-on capability in Microsoft ecosystems, SQL, Azure, ETL/ELT pipelines, and Power BI.

Understanding of APIs, integrations, and workflow automation

A passion for agentic AI, automation, and improving how people work.

Experience in manufacturing, construction, fabrication, or supply chain environments (preferred)

You enjoy both long-term strategy and rolling up your sleeves to build.

 You exhibit professional traits, including:

Exhibiting excellent judgment and high standards

Thinking strategically, but staying on top of tactical execution

Thinking big and standing firm in your convictions

Being results-oriented

Developing great people and being able to inspire passion in others

 Why Join Us?

Exciting opportunity: drive transformation for a growing business delivering superior results.

Great team culture: Join a positive, collaborative work environment that is strongly values-driven.

State-of-the-art facilities: Work in a modern, cutting-edge building alongside a skilled, multidisciplinary team.

Seeking a Senior Data Scientist to join our Design team. 

$190,026 base salary plus superannuation

Permanent FT | Haymarket. Grow your career with us today.  

  

About Ausgrid – we’re wired for good 

We’re the largest distributer of electricity on Australia’s east coast, which means over two million homes and businesses rely on our good work. We’re here at the heart of the energy transition, and we’re driven by a powerful purpose to make electricity accessible for all. 

It’s an exciting new chapter for our company with each day bringing a renewed energy to how we deliver, store and use electricity. As we continue to grow, we’re in search of exceptional talent to help us achieve our goals.  

Everyone’s job is important here. Our customers come from all walks of life, and so do we. We’re committed to fostering a diverse, respectful, and inclusive workplace, and encourage applications from all backgrounds. 

  

See yourself working here 

At Ausgrid, we’re building intelligent, data driven solutions that help power communities across NSW. We’re looking for a Senior Data Scientist to design, develop and deploy AI and advanced analytics solutions that directly support our business objectives and integrate seamlessly into our enterprise data landscape. You will be based at our Haymarket office and reporting to Manager of Data Services and Platforms.  

This is a hands-on role for someone who enjoys solving complex problems, working end to end across the AI lifecycle, and partnering closely with the business to deliver real outcomes. 

You’ll collaborate with stakeholders, testers and SMEs, translating business use cases into robust AI solutions that create measurable value. 

This a unique role to work on Work on meaningful AI solutions that support critical infrastructure and communities and work for a business that is at the heart of the energy transition. 

 

Key responsibilities 

AI & Analytics Development: Design, build, train and deploy ML and LLM based models to address specific business use cases, integrating with existing systems and platforms. 

MLOps & Deployment: Leverage Ausgrid’s data platform and established MLOps/LLMOps practices to operationalise models at scale. 

Performance & Monitoring: Implement monitoring, alerting and cost management routines to assess model performance and reliability. 

Data Modelling & Serving: Build Lakehouse aligned data models (Bronze/Silver/Gold), optimising for performance, reliability and cost. 

Security & Governance: Apply strong data security controls, encryption, secrets management and audit logging, in line with governance and regulatory requirements. 

CI/CD & IaC: Contribute to CI/CD pipelines, version control and environment promotion using Azure DevOps/Git and infrastructureascode (Bicep). 

Operations & Support: Monitor production jobs and SLAs, perform root cause analysis and work with support teams to resolve issues. 

Collaboration & Documentation: Engage with stakeholders throughout delivery and produce clear technical documentation, runbooks and data dictionaries. 

 

What you’ll bring  

Our hiring decisions are not only based on your experience and skills, but your passion, attitude, and alignment to our core values.  

 

Essential experience and skills 

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering or a related discipline. 

Strong software development experience using Python, Spark and SQL. 

Proven experience working with Azure and largescale, cloud based data workflows. 

Solid database design and optimisation experience (e.g. PostgreSQL). 

Working knowledge of machine learning workflows in Databricks. 

Experience with automated unit and integration testing. 

Familiarity with Agile delivery and MLOps practices. 

Strong full stack mindset with the ability to learn quickly and deliver scalable, high quality solutions. 

  

Life at Ausgrid  

At Ausgrid, we go beyond just a competitive salary to offer an exciting range of benefits. From salary sacrificing electric vehicles and novated leasing, to discounted corporate health insurance and access to Fitness Passport, our benefits support your work here, and your life out there.  

Celebrate success every day with our employee recognition program to bring out each other’s best. Looking to grow professionally? We’ve got you covered with study assistance, secondments, training and mentors, whose advice you’ll keep wherever you go.  We’re proud to offer gender-neutral parental leave to balance work when growing your family. And if you’re caring for elderly parents, our aged care support services offer expert guidance to help you access care for your loved ones. At Ausgrid, it's all about supporting you in every aspect of life. 

  

If you require support with your application/reasonable adjustments for the interview process, please reach out to our dedicated Talent Team recruitment@ausgrid.com.au.

At the National Rugby League (NRL), we’re incredibly proud of our game and the impact it has on players, fans, and communities across Australia and the Pacific. Rugby League is an inclusive sport for everyone, and our workplace reflects that we are inclusive, celebrate diversity, and strive to create a culture where all people feel they belong. Rugby League brings people together both on and off the field, with our broadcasts consistently ranking among the most‑watched programs nationwide.
  
We’re looking for an Infrastructure Engineer (Network) to join our Technology Team and help shape the future of Rugby League. If you’re ready to make an impact in a fast-paced, purpose-led organisation, we want to hear from you.
  
About the role:
As an Infrastructure Engineer (Network), you’ll play a key role in shaping and supporting the NRL’s hybrid technology environment—powering the digital experiences of millions of fans across Australia. You’ll design, implement, and maintain critical infrastructure spanning both modern cloud platforms and essential legacy systems, ensuring reliability, performance, and security across all environments.
  
This role is central to the NRL’s digital transformation, giving you the opportunity to bring deep technical expertise, uplift infrastructure capability, and help drive the evolution of our technology landscape.
  
 In the role you will:  

- Maintain and optimise the NRL’s critical infrastructure platforms, including digital identity, content delivery, data lakehouse, and API environments.
- Manage cloud and on‑premises infrastructure across AWS, Azure, networking, servers, and telephony to ensure high availability and performance.
- Design, operate, and secure network and telecommunications systems, including firewalls, WAN/LAN, and voice services.
- Implement modern cloud infrastructure using IaC, containerisation, CI/CD tooling, and observability platforms.
- Strengthen security posture through Zero Trust principles, vulnerability management, compliance frameworks, and incident response.
- Deliver advanced technical support, troubleshooting complex infrastructure issues and collaborating with internal teams and vendors.
- Lead and contribute to infrastructure projects, cloud migrations, and modernisation initiatives aligned with strategic technology goals.
- Maintain robust backup, disaster recovery, and business continuity processes with thorough documentation
  
Ideally you will have:

- Extensive experience (8+ years) across infrastructure and security, with a strong track record leading cloud transformation and modernisation initiatives.
- Deep capability across hybrid environments, including telephony, on‑prem networks, server management, endpoint security, and cloud platforms (AWS/Azure).
- Strong networking expertise spanning firewalls, routing, switching, and the transition to modern cloud‑networking architectures.
- Proven experience in modern Infrastructure principals and experience in IaC (Terraform, CloudFormation) and containerisation (Docker, Kubernetes) or similar systems.
- Proven security leadership, including Zero Trust, IAM, vulnerability management, incident response, and compliance frameworks such as ISO 27001, SOC 2, and NIST.
- Robust knowledge of DevOps architecture and alignment in CI/CD integration, automation, configuration management, and cloud‑native application patterns..
- Strong operational discipline across DR/BCP, backup strategies, performance optimisation, cost management, and vendor coordination.
- Excellent communication, stakeholder engagement, and change‑leadership skills, with the ability to guide teams through technology uplift and organisational transformation
  
  
Whilst we look for experience, we also look for people who share the same beliefs as our organisation – we are UNITED, POSITIVE, INCLUSIVE & DISCIPLINED.  Should you meet most of the criteria and share our beliefs, please apply.
  
The NRL is a child-safe organisation committed to providing a safe place for all children to learn and have fun. We have strong recruitment procedures to make sure the safest and most suitable people work with us and require all applicants to undergo an extensive screening process prior to appointment, a process that will include, but is not limited to comprehensive reference checks, an identity check, “Working with Children” checks and/or national criminal history checks.

The Data & Integration Engineer plays a critical role within Moco’s IT team, sitting at the intersection of technology and the business. Based in Wacol and reporting to the Capability Development Manager, this role owns the design, development and continuous improvement of system integrations, data flows and reporting across the organisation.

 

From managing customer and supplier EDI integrations through to designing scalable system architecture and improving reporting capability, this role ensures the right information moves to the right place at the right time. By partnering closely with operational and commercial teams, the Data & Integration Engineer helps translate real business needs into practical and reliable technical solutions.

 

 

About You

 

You are someone who enjoys solving complex problems and building systems that work seamlessly together. You bring strong technical capability, particularly in SQL and systems integration, but equally enjoy working with stakeholders to understand operational challenges and turn them into scalable solutions.

 

You thrive in environments where technology enables improvement. Whether it is automating processes, improving data reliability, or designing smarter integrations, you enjoy delivering outcomes that make the business run better.

 

Skills, Experience & Attributes

 

·         Strong SQL (DB2,Mssql) capability including query optimisation and data manipulation.

·         Experience designing and supporting systems integrations across multiple platforms.

·         Experience working with data pipelines, ETL processes.

·         Experience supporting both internal and external system integrations.

·         Experience managing or supporting EDI integrations with customers and suppliers.

·         Hands-on experience with reporting and data platforms such as Power BI.

·         Experience working with GoAnywhere MFT is highly regarded.

·         Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving capability.

·         Ability to translate business requirements into practical technical solutions.

·         Comfortable working with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

 

Benefits

 

·         Work within Moco’s brand new, state-of-the-art facility in Wacol, with plenty of on-site parking available

·         Collaborative and friendly workplace environment, with weekly cross-departmental events

·         Join a growing company and use your expertise to make a real impact on the business

·         Leadership training and development opportunities available for aspiring employees

·         Access to great discounts on over 6,500 product items for current employees.

·         Enjoy free access to financial advice, home loan support, and exclusive lifestyle perks & discounts through our partnership with Commonwealth Bank.
